final:
// // This module simulates an EEPROM memory cell with a data input (D), a read enable (RE), a write enable (WE), and a clock (CLK). When WE is high, the data input should be written to the memory cell on the falling edge of the clock. When RE is high, the data stored in the memory cell should be output on the data output (Q). If both RE and WE are low, the memory cell should retain its value.
module eeprom (
    input wire CLK,
    input wire WE,
    input wire RE,
    input wire [7:0] D,
    output reg [7:0] Q
);
    reg [7:0] memory;

    always @(posedge CLK) begin
        if (WE) begin
            memory <= D; // Write data to memory
        end
    end

    always @(*) begin
        if (RE) begin
            Q <= memory; // Read data from memory
        end else begin
            Q <= Q; // Hold previous data
        end
    end
endmodule

When WE is high, the data input should be written to the memory cell on the falling edge of the clock. When RE is high, the data stored in the memory cell should be output on the data output (Q). If both RE and WE are low, the memory cell should retain its value.
